daily_summary.headline: Exciting Aurora and Routine Launches Await

top_events.title: G2 Aurora Storm Expected
top_events.description: A G2-level geomagnetic storm is predicted, offering excellent aurora viewing opportunities for observers at latitudes down to 55°.
top_events.action: Prepare for aurora viewing if skies are clear.

top_events.title: Falcon 9 Starlink Launch
top_events.description: SpaceX's Falcon 9 will launch another batch of Starlink satellites, continuing to expand the global internet constellation.
top_events.action: Watch the live stream for the launch.

top_events.title: Electron "Eight Days A Week" Launch
top_events.description: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket will carry a synthetic aperture radar satellite for Synspective, aiding in Earth observation.

alerts.title: G2 Aurora Storm Alert
alerts.message: A G2-level geomagnetic storm is expected on March 19, 2026, providing excellent aurora viewing opportunities for observers at latitudes down to 55°.
alerts.action_required: Prepare for aurora viewing if skies are clear.

opportunities.recommendation: Good viewing opportunity for high-latitude observers. Worth checking if you're above 60° latitude and skies are clear.
Good viewing opportunity for high-latitude observers. Worth checking if you're above 60° latitude and skies are clear.
Possible aurora at high latitudes. Best for northern locations (65°+). Worth checking if conditions are optimal.
